title: T-004 — permissions.enum.ts source-of-truth lock with org scope, fg permissions, and SoD pair
prompt:
# T-004 — permissions.enum.ts source-of-truth lock with org scope, fg permissions, and SoD pair

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §3, §13, §W0-v4.3
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
ACP import shape: top-level TaskCreate only (`title`, `prompt`, `labels`, `priority`, `max_attempts`, `pipeline_name`, `pipeline_inputs`). Do not add generated ACP fields such as task_id/status/project_id. `pipeline_inputs.root_path` is mandatory.

## Goal
Lock RBAC permission strings for org-scoped business access, including Org Admin / Schema Admin separation-of-duties and canonical `fg.*` permissions.

## Locked Wave 0 decisions to honor
- Business data-plane scope is `org_id`; do not create new business-table `tenant_id` columns. Tenant/control-plane concepts may exist above org scope, but app/business rows, RLS policies, audit/outbox rows, role grants, and R13 skeletons use `org_id`.
- Canonical finished-good domain/event prefix is `fg.*`. `fa.*` / `FA` may appear only as an explicit legacy compatibility alias during migration, never as the primary new contract.
- Use the safe non-spoofable org-context RLS pattern. Do not rely on unsafe direct custom GUC `SET` by app users. Do not declare `LEAKPROOF` unless the implementation proves it is valid and necessary.
- Lower numeric priority is picked sooner; dependencies must be local `T-XXX` IDs. Cross-module blockers, if any, belong in `pipeline_inputs.cross_module_dependencies` and in this prompt.
- Exactly one atomic task type: `T1-schema`.

## Implementation contract
Create `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` exporting `Permission` constants: `org.access.admin`, `org.schema.admin`, `org.scim.write`, `fg.create`, `fg.edit`, `brief.convert_to_npd_project`, `ref.edit`, `audit.read`, `outbox.admin`, `impersonate.org`. Export `LegacyPermissionAlias` mapping `fa.create -> fg.create`, `fa.edit -> fg.edit`, and `brief.convert_to_fa -> brief.convert_to_npd_project` for compatibility only. Export `ALL_PERMISSIONS` with canonical values only and `SOD_EXCLUSIVE_PAIRS = [['org.access.admin','org.schema.admin']]`. Add `normalizePermission()` helper and CODEOWNERS lock. This task is enum-only; enforcement is T-014/T-050.

## Files in scope
- packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts [create]
- packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts [create]
- CODEOWNERS [modify]

## Dependencies
- T-001

## Cross-module dependencies
- None

## Acceptance criteria
1. ALL_PERMISSIONS has no duplicates and every value matches /^[a-z]+(\.[a-z_]+)+$/.
2. SOD_EXCLUSIVE_PAIRS contains exactly ['org.access.admin','org.schema.admin'].
3. ALL_PERMISSIONS contains `fg.create`, `fg.edit`, and `brief.convert_to_npd_project`; it does not contain `fa.create`, `fa.edit`, or `brief.convert_to_fa`.
4. normalizePermission('brief.convert_to_fa') returns `brief.convert_to_npd_project` and normalizePermission('fa.edit') returns `fg.edit`.

## Test strategy
- RED: write permissions.test.ts covering format, SoD pair, fg canonical permissions, and legacy alias normalization before enum implementation.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/rbac test` and typecheck if available.

## Risk red lines
- Do not allow legacy aliases in ALL_PERMISSIONS.
- Do not introduce tenant-scoped business permissions; use org-scoped names.
- Do not implement grant enforcement in this enum task.

## Closeout evidence
Provide changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status, deviations_from_prd, and git_status.
